title: dryopteris affinis subsp. paleaceolobata
intro: Dryopteris affinis subsp. paleaceolobata, also known as the golden-scaled male fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus. It is an edible and medicinal plant traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. The medicinal properties of the golden-scaled male fern include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or dried extracts to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
